mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals improved labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.2pp in 1 month, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns falls below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in INDPRO up periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC drops below 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal accommodative monetary policy and disinflation, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations for positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in co-declining FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL periods are negative or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the federal funds rate during a tightening cycle raise borrowing costs, dampening equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in FEDFUNDS rising periods are positive or the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX returns exceeds -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Growing housing starts (HOUST) reflect increased consumer demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in HOUST up periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in CPIAUCSL above 2.5% signal inflationary pressures, prompting central bank rate hikes that compress equity earnings multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% periods are positive or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in PAYEMS and declines in UNRATE indicate a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in co-occurring PAYEMS/UNRATE regimes are negative or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic expansion, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in GDPC1 up periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.
